title: Intsia bijuga
taxonomy: Intsia bijuga is a species of tree in the family Fabaceae. It is native to Southeast Asia, and its classification has been confirmed through botanical research. Further classification details include it being part of the genus Intsia, which is a type of legume tree. Intsia bijuga is also known as the kamuning tree in some regions. The classification of Intsia bijuga is essential for understanding its ecological role and potential uses in forestry and horticulture.

The bark and roots of Intsia bijuga contain tannins, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. intro: Intsia bijuga, also known as the breadfruit tree, is a large, evergreen tree native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia. The health benefits of Intsia bijuga include its ability to act as an anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial agent, which can aid in wound healing and protect against infections.
